mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising consumer credit (RSAFS) and elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates overheating demand that forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, which historically precedes equity drawdowns as valuation multiples contract.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative 60d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 5 rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-capacity utilization environment, a sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals supply chain bottlenecks resolving into demand weakness, leading to earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 1.5% month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not correlate with negative 60d SPX returns in walk-forward testing (IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Rising real estate construction starts (HOUST) reflect strong business and consumer confidence, which historically leads positive S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 5d returns in >55% of observations over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%, the cost of capital becomes sufficiently restrictive to pressure highly valued growth stocks, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of strong employment growth (PAYEMS) and robust industrial output (INDPRO) confirms a self-reinforcing economic expansion that sustains corporate profit margins, generating positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict positive 20d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 5 rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid economic acceleration, a sharp deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) acts as an early warning for earnings revisions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 deceleration fails to predict negative 60d returns in walk-forward testing (IC < 0.02).
